sections.paragraph:
ἀναφωνέω 1 aor. ἀνεφώνησα (s. φωνέω; Epicurus 63, 28 [p. 24, 16 Us.]; Polyb. 3, 33, 4; Ps.-Aristot., De Mundo 6 p. 400a, 18; Artem. I, 58; UPZ 162 V, 26; PFay 14, 2 [both II b.c.]; 1 Ch 15:28 al.; 2 Ch 5:13; TestJob 31:7) cry out, ἀ. κραυγῇ μεγάλῃ cry out loudly Lk 1:42 (s. ἀναβοάω)—DELG s.v. φωνή. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

Epicurus Epicurus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Ps.-Aristot Ps.-Aristot , s. Aristot.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 75
